title: Universal Data Interchange Format (UDIF) 3.0 — Generative Human Intelligence (GHI)
description: A schema for portable, structured Generative Human Intelligence records. GHI data represents perspective, judgment, and interpretive knowledge elicited from human subjects. The GHI taxonomy is defined by the PAVE Protocol; UDIF defines the interchange format for portability and interoperability.

meta.description: Metadata describing the origin and consent context of the GHI record

source.description: How the GHI data was elicited (e.g., structured interview, guided reflection session)

generator.description: Tool or model that produced the structured record from raw elicitation

subject_id.description: Identifier for the human subject whose GHI is captured

consent_granted.description: Whether the subject granted consent for this record

consent_type.description: Type of consent obtained from the subject

pave_protocol_reference.description: Reference URL for the PAVE Protocol specification that defines the GHI taxonomy

ghi_record.description: The core GHI data containing perspective, judgment, and interpretive knowledge

domain.description: Primary domains of expertise relevant to this record

time_period.description: The time period this GHI record covers

start.description: ISO 8601 date marking the start of the relevant period

end.description: ISO 8601 date marking the end of the relevant period, or 'present'

context.description: Situational description of the environment or conditions in which the GHI was generated

perspective_data.description: How the subject sees — the distinctive lens shaped by their experience

distinctive_lens.description: The unique viewpoint or frame the subject brings to this domain

what_I_saw_others_didnt.description: Specific insight or observation the subject identified that others in the same context missed

judgment_data.description: How the subject acts on what they know — decisions, stakes, reasoning, and outcomes

decision.description: The specific decision the subject made

stakes.description: What was at risk when the decision was made

reasoning.description: The reasoning process behind the decision

consequence.description: What happened as a result of the decision

learned.description: What the subject learned from the outcome

interpretive_data.description: How the subject makes sense of what they see — frameworks, principles, and transferable patterns

framework_emerged.description: A mental model or framework that emerged from the subject's experience, or null if none crystallized

principle_crystallized.description: A guiding principle the subject distilled from their experience

transferable_pattern.description: A pattern the subject identified that applies beyond the original context

provenance.description: Cryptographic metadata establishing origin, authenticity, and integrity of the GHI record

source.description: System or process that produced this record

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the ghi_record object for integrity verification

signature.description: Cryptographic signature from the subject proving authorship and consent

chain.description: Auditable history of every system this record has moved through

hash.description: Hash of data at point of export, enabling tamper detection

schema_gaps.description: Gaps flagged during extraction where the schema could not fully capture the subject's knowledge. These are observations, not invented fields.
